response.justification: Bogdan presents a credible staff-level profile with genuine distributed systems depth from his Dunnhumby/Sociomantic tenure (millions of HTTP requests per second, low-latency async I/O) and is actively working in an AI-native engineering environment at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure. However, Python is a stated must-have and is entirely absent from the resume and skills list. The high-throughput systems work is 6+ years old and more recent roles have been full-stack product work. Explicit evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management, formal incident response, and on-call operational ownership is also thin.
strengths: Proven high-throughput distributed systems experience: micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second with low-latency async I/O at Dunnhumby (2018–2020)
Current hands-on AI-native engineering: MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure at re:cinq, directly matching the role's AI-driven development baseline
Consistent staff-level seniority and breadth across 15+ years with ownership evidence including open-source platform authorship, workshops, and founding an active GIS platform
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume and skills list — a stated must-have technical skill for the role
The directly relevant high-throughput distributed systems work is from 2018–2020; the past five years have been predominantly full-stack product and tooling work at smaller scale
No explicit evidence of formal on-call rotations, incident response processes, or cross-functional stakeholder management at the scope expected for a staff IC driving technical strategy
